question_text: A student makes an electromagnet by wrapping wire around an iron nail and connecting it to a battery. She then wraps the same wire around the nail 50 more times. What effect will adding more coils have on the electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: The electromagnet will become weaker because the wire is thinner

options.label: B
options.text: The electromagnet will become stronger because there are more coils to create a magnetic field

options.label: C
options.text: The electromagnet will stay the same strength because the battery voltage hasn't changed

options.label: D
options.text: The electromagnet will stop working because too much wire will overheat

explanation: Adding more coils to an electromagnet increases the number of loops the current flows through, which creates a stronger magnetic field. Each coil contributes to the overall magnetism, so more coils means a stronger electromagnet.
